entries.notes: Noun form of 帰る; used in expressions like 帰りに (on the way home).
⚠ False ichidan: ends in える but conjugates as godan (帰って not 帰て).
⚠ False ichidan: きる looks ichidan but conjugates as godan (切って not 切て). Compare 着る (きる, ichidan, to wear) → 着て.
Direction: someone gives something to the speaker or in-group. Contrast with あげる (speaker gives to out-group).
First-person singular pronoun. Neutral politeness level suitable for most situations.
Noun form of the color red. Adjective form is 赤い (v_akai).
Noun form of the color blue. Adjective form is 青い (v_aoi).
Noun form of the color brown. Adjective form is 茶色い (v_chairoi).
Noun form of the color black. Adjective form is 黒い (v_kuroi).
Noun expressing manner or resemblance. Xのように = like X / in the manner of X. Xのようだ = seems like X. Used with の after nouns, and directly after plain-form verbs. 犬のように走る = run like a dog.
Used in the comparison construction AのほうがBより (A is more ... than B). Indicates one side or option being compared. Written in hiragana (ほう) until 方 kanji is taught (N4.26).

Auxiliary adjective expressing resemblance or manner. Casual equivalent of のよう. Attaches directly to nouns without の. みたいに = in a manner like X (adverbial); みたいな + Noun = a noun that is like X (attributive). Do not confuse with the conjecture use (みたいだ = 'it seems like') taught later in G33.

entries.meaning: to give (outward — speaker/in-group gives to someone else)
entries.notes: Direction: speaker or in-group gives to an outsider. Contrast with くれる (someone gives TO the speaker) and もらう (speaker receives). て-form あげて is used in てあげる (do something as a favour for someone).

The receiver is the subject. The source is marked with に (close relationship) or から (more distance/abstract). て-form もらって is used in てもらう (have someone do something for you / receive the favor of an action).
体 (N4.6) + 力 (N4.3). Also used as RPG/game stat.
Used when referring to one's own older brother. Polite/referring to others' older brother: お兄さん (v_oniisan).
Used when referring to one's own older sister. Polite/referring to others' older sister: お姉さん (v_oneesan).
Na-adjective. 親切な人 (kind person); 親切です (is kind). Taught as 親切 (shinsetsu).
Refers to one's own parent(s). Plain/informal. Polite forms: お父さん, お母さん.
Used when referring to one's own younger sister. Polite/referring to others' younger sister: 妹さん.
Used when referring to one's own younger brother. Polite/referring to others' younger brother: 弟さん.

entries.meaning: Yes! / I did it! (casual exclamation of joy)
entries.notes: Casual exclamation of delight or success. Plain past of やる (to do), used as a standalone interjection.

entries.meaning: to give (honorific) / to do (for me/us) — honorific form of くれる
entries.notes: Honorific counterpart of くれる. Used when a social superior (teacher, senior, boss) does something for you or gives you something. Te-form: くださって. Polite: くださいます (irregular ます-form).
Kanji 季節 not introduced until N3; always written in kana at N4/N5 level.
Kanji 緑 not introduced until N3; always written in kana at N4/N5 level.

entries.readingAlign: 春(しゅん)夏(か)秋(しゅう)冬(とう)

entries.notes: Kanji 涼しい not in curriculum; always written in kana.
Kanji 雪 is not introduced at this level; always written in kana.
Polite form of 元気 with honorific お prefix
Loanword from English 'memo'. Used as a noun (メモを書く = take a note) or as a suru-verb (メモする = to jot down).

Used as the noun in ために (in order to / for the sake of). The kanji 為 is not in the taught set; always write in hiragana as ため. In jp fields tag as v_tame when the token ため appears before に as a purpose connector.
Katakana loanword for the United States of America.
Katakana loanword for England / the UK. Closely related to the kanji 英 (introduced in N4.10).
撮 is an N1 kanji — always write as とる in lesson content
Used as both noun (早口だ) and na-adjective (早口な人).
Kanji 忙しい not introduced until N3; always written in kana at this level.

entries.meaning: to put / to place / to leave (as is) / to do in advance (auxiliary)
entries.notes: As auxiliary (〜ておく): do something in advance or leave a result in place. 置 kanji unlocks at N3.20 — kanji form 置く is also tagged here via matches[].

entries.meaning: to put away / to finish up / to end up doing (auxiliary, often with regret)
entries.notes: As auxiliary (〜てしまう): express completion or accidental/regrettable action. Casual contraction: 〜ちゃう (after て-form ending in て) / 〜じゃう (after ending in で). Kanji 仕舞 not in taught set; always write as しまう.

Abstract nominalizer for intangible things, matters, or experiences. Conventionally written in hiragana even though the kanji 事 exists. Key grammar uses: Xのことが好き (to like X / everything about X), ～ことがある (have the experience of), ～ことにする (decide to), ～ことができる (can do).
Often refers to a professional or specific type of person.
Often used for 'person' in a relative clause or humble context.
Kanji 疲れる not introduced until N3; always written in kana at N4/N5 level.
⚠ False ichidan: ends in しる but conjugates as godan (走って not 走て).
Often used as noun modifier (自動の).
Often used for feelings or memories coming and going.

entries.meaning: I see / that makes sense / indeed
entries.notes: Aizuchi (active listening signal). Signals genuine comprehension — the listener has understood and processed what was said. Slightly formal; appropriate in most registers.
Aizuchi expressing that the speaker has just received new or surprising information. Signals interest and engagement. Neutral-to-casual register.

entries.meaning: Yes, exactly! / right, right! / that's it!
entries.notes: Enthusiastic aizuchi for agreement or confirmation. More emphatic than そうですね. Casual register. Triple form そうそうそう is even more emphatic.

entries.meaning: actually / to tell you the truth / as a matter of fact
entries.notes: Discourse restructuring marker. Signals that what follows may contradict expectations or reveal something new. 実 unlocks at N3.18; both じつは and 実は tag to this entry via matches[].

entries.meaning: in other words / that is to say / to put it simply
entries.notes: Discourse restructuring marker. Signals a restatement, summary, or simplification of what was previously said. Used when clarifying or getting to the point.

entries.meaning: like / kind of / you know (casual filler)
entries.notes: Casual speech filler. Extremely common in younger casual speech as a hesitation marker or softener. Reads as careless or immature in professional or formal contexts — avoid in polite speech.

entries.meaning: well / sort of / I suppose / mm
entries.notes: Softens assertions and expresses resigned acceptance or mild uncertainty. More register-neutral than なんか. まあ、そうですね = Well, I suppose so. Can also express mild surprise (まあ！).
Used as an aizuchi to emphasise that the listener is paying close attention. ほんとうに！ = 'Really!' / ほんとう？ = 'Is that so?' Kanji form 本当に — 当 is not introduced in this curriculum; always write as ほんとうに.
思 (N4.30) + 出 (N5.17). Noun form of 思い出す (to recall). いい思い出 = good memory. Both kanji are taught by N4.30; always write 思い出 from this lesson onward.
Kanji 難 is JLPT N3. Move entry to N3 glossary when N3 content is created. Likely to be removed from N4.17.

entries.meaning: to drive around / to ride around

entries.notes: Kanji 寺 is JLPT N3. Move entry to N3 glossary when N3 content is created. Likely to be removed from N4.17.
Common surname (Lit. 'Rice field by the pond').

entries.readingAlign: 有(ゆう)力(りょく)者(しゃ)

entries.meaning: to take out / to carry out
to take home / to carry back
to hold / to have / to possess
to be acceptable / to be okay (used in grammar construction てはいけない — must not / not okay to)
entries.notes: Functional verb in the grammar construction てはいけない (te-form + は + いけない). The plain negative いけない and polite negative いけません express prohibition. Taught in G21.
Loanword from English 'flea market'. A market where people sell used or secondhand goods.
Polite form of 客 (きゃく). The お- prefix and さん suffix make it polite/honorific. Common in service contexts.
Used as a determiner: ほかの～ (other ~). Also appears in ほかに (besides / in addition) and ほかにも (also; other things too). The kanji form 他 is not in the taught set at this level; always write in hiragana.
Used as an example foreign surname (written in katakana). Illustrates how foreign names appear in katakana/romaji in Japanese writing contexts.

entries.meaning: to gain weight / to grow fat

entries.readingAlign: 研(けん)究(きゅう)者(しゃ)

entries.notes: Used as a suffix for 'how to do X' (e.g., 書き方).

entries.meaning: to fit / to suit / to match
all at once / at one time

entries.notes: Usually used as 心待ちにする (to look forward to).

entries.meaning: how many times / how many degrees

entries.notes: Someone outside the field of expertise.
Often used for beginner books (e.g., 'Introduction to X').
Used as a prefix for direction/comparison (e.g., above, below).
Often used to describe a person's nature.

entries.meaning: with the exception of / other than

entries.notes: Often used in te-form (知っています) for state of knowing. ⚠ False ichidan: ends in しる but conjugates as godan (知って not 知て).

entries.meaning: to be conceited / to be arrogant

entries.notes: Na-adjective (特別な). Compounds: 特 (N4.30) + 別 (N4.9)

entries.meaning: feature (e.g. in a magazine) / special edition

entries.notes: Often used as 堂々とした (modifying noun) or 堂々と (adverb).

entries.meaning: to open (a book/event) / to hold (an event)
to substitute / to replace / to change

entries.notes: Intransitive. 変わる = to change (state), distinct from 代わる = to substitute.
Used as a suffix (e.g. 東京市).
Often used as a suffix indicating purpose (e.g. 子供用 - for children).

entries.meaning: to spread / to propagate / to widen

entries.notes: Na-adjective (広大な). Compounds: 広 (N4.33) + 大 (N5.9)

entries.meaning: to be built / to be erected

entries.notes: Used as suffix for departure time/place.

entries.meaning: to commute / to go back and forth
to let pass / to pass through

entries.notes: Godan verb. Object can be hands, dishes, car, clothes, etc.

entries.meaning: citizen / national / the people of a nation

entries.notes: Compounds: 民 (N4.35) + 家 (N5). Contrasts with 公共施設 (public facilities).
Standalone reading ぬし; as prefix しゅ in compounds.
Standalone reading たみ; as prefix みん in compounds.
Compounds: 手 (N5) + 洗 (N4.35). Polite word for toilet/bathroom.

entries.meaning: to stop / to come to a halt (intransitive)

entries.notes: E.g., バスの便 (bus service), 航空便 (airmail). The kanji 便 (N4.36) read as びん.
Compounds: 不 (N4.36) + 便 (N4.36). Opposite of 便利.
Used as a standalone noun or as a suffix. E.g., 工業, 産業, 農業.
Compounds: 不 (N4.36) + 味 (N4.6). Also used figuratively for awkward situations.
Godan verb. Used for giving birth to a child or producing something.
Casual attention-getter used at the start of a sentence to call someone's attention or invite a response. Common in informal speech among close friends.
Informal name suffix, typically used for boys or younger males. Less formal than さん.
Attaches to pronouns or names to indicate a group: 私たち (we), 子どもたち (children).
Intransitive verb meaning 'to be heard / to be audible'. Unlike 聞く (to listen), 聞こえる describes sounds that reach the listener naturally.
Godan verb. Kanji 驚く not taught until N1, so written in hiragana.
